audit: unauthorized-behavior-change sweep, base 3fa7ad7a -> 9730b628 (original implementation diff), fix wave 9730b628..6c9e9a9e treated as authorized context (D17-D20)
method: code->authority: every production behavioral delta in the 40-file diff traced to Key Decisions 1-20 / KTD1-8 / R1-R33 / AE1-7 / U1-U11 text, the issue #711 thread record, or the validated review record. A rationale comment is not authority.

residual_risks: Gate apply-failure semantics changed without a named decision: a finalize_request failure after a successful import now surfaces as outcome='failed' ('unhandled exception in auto-import') with a self-heal requeue, while the album is actually in beets. Direction is forced by R4/D19 (no silent terminal parking) and matches the house self-heal doctrine, but the audit-row wording will read oddly for this shape; no test pins it (also a review-recorded gap: 'a raised decision must reopen, not silently import - no test').

Post-import requeue override moved from the explicit upgrade-tier CSV to NULL: authorized by R4/AE4/R27, but operators should know every retained lossy import now searches config defaults WITH the catch-all lane, where base pinned six named tiers with catch-all off. resolve_user_requeue_override still hands user-initiated requeues the legacy CSV - a deliberate asymmetry worth an eventual doc note.

Every snapshot-fingerprint change (retag, art embed) now costs one have_analysis_error attempt before preview enrichment self-heals (the _current_action_missing_enrichment_reasons gate). Review deployment notes quantified this only for converted rows with NULL spectral; it applies to any stale-snapshot HAVE.

have_analysis_error's to_wanted transition applies to force/manual attempts too (U2's letter): a 'manual'-status request whose operator-initiated import hits a failed HAVE analysis moves to 'wanted'. Settled text covers it ('Return the request to ordinary wanted behavior') but the operator-facing effect on manual-review rows is worth awareness.

verified_lossless_locked's preserve_imported writes RequestTransition.to_imported() from whatever status the request held - for the 1-row wanted-with-proof reconciliation cohort, the first automatic candidate auto-completes the request to terminal imported (R1-consistent; supplements the deploy-window reconciliation glance).

Review-recorded residuals still open and unchanged by this audit: expected_current_evidence_id=0 magic sentinel; R9 fresh-HAVE gate bypassed when attempt_result.audit is None (legacy/no-preview dispatch paths); library-row proofs uniformly 'carried' collapsing the measured/carried Recents distinction; staged-dir leftovers can wedge subsequent attempts into repeated fail-closed mismatches.
coverage_notes: Primary diff walked file-by-file: all 40 production files in the 3fa7ad7a..9730b628 stat were read as full diffs (cratedigger.py, harness/import_one.py, all 8 lib/dispatch files, download_validation, import_evidence, import_preview, measurement, 3 lib/pipeline_db files, all 10 lib/quality files, quality_evidence, sidecar, terminal_outcomes x2, wrong_match_cleanup_service, migrations 054+055, nix/module.nix, 2 scripts/pipeline_cli files, import_preview_worker, web/classify, web/download_history_view, 3 web/js files).

Test deltas in modules outside the review appendix's flip enumeration were scanned as pointers (test_force_import_gates, test_download, test_import_one_stages, test_conversion_e2e, test_wrong_match_cleanup_service, test_sidecar*, test_measurement, test_import_evidence, tests/web/*, _mock_audit_scanner, test_replaced_write_audit): every behavioral pointer resolved to an inventoried production delta. The quality_decision_stage test edits (adding format/avg to bare measurements) reflect the R27 UNKNOWN-equivalence branch, not an untraced change.

The wrong-match backfilled-proof flip (test_backfilled_verified_lossless_does_not_short_circuit -> _routes_through_reducer) was traced to R18+D6 rather than any wrong_match_cleanup_service logic change (the loaded-only shortcut gate is byte-identical at base and branch; verified by side-by-side file read). Confidence 75: the exact base bail mechanism (bool/proof validation mismatch) was inferred from the struct validation rules, not re-executed.

Fix-wave commits (9730b628..6c9e9a9e) were read for messages and spot-verified in the working tree (D17 subject-blind narrowing, D18 no-blame reopen, D19 both force gates removed including the retained-import mapping, D20 cooldown unification, 055 converted-row carve-out, preview crash guard, verified_lossless_locked renderer) - not re-audited per instructions.

No test module was executed; the audit is static per the brief. The 3 unauthorized changes in the original diff (findings #1, #2/#10, #8 + its second door) and the 055 seeding defect (#3) were all previously caught and are remediated at HEAD; this sweep found no additional unauthorized behavioral delta.

One imprecision in the prior review corrected for the record: finding #8's validator note said 'denylist still fires on these paths' - true for rejection decisions, but the original diff DID drop the base denylist for force/manual retained imports (transcode_upgrade/provisional) because the flag moved onto the automatic-gated mapping. D19's fix (ea1a6dc9) restores it, so there is no residual defect - but the note under-described the original blast radius.
